reason:
Agent attempted to use GitHub tool to retrieve username but failed due to 401 authentication error - response did not include GitHub username
  Bugs found:
    - GitHub OAuth authentication failed: Agent received "401 Bad credentials" error when attempting to call GitHub API endpoint (api.github.com/user) through the platform proxy. The injected OAuth token was not valid or not being injected properly into the GitHub REST API requests, preventing the agent from successfully retrieving the authenticated user's GitHub username.
steps: 1. Navigated to http://localhost:47891 — Page loaded successfully with agent list visible
2. Found and clicked agent "QA-20260507-211129-27ig" in sidebar — Agent detail page opened with session creation
3. Verified agent status — Status displayed as "idle" ✓
4. Sent message "Use the GitHub tool to check who I am. Tell me my GitHub username." — Message was submitted and appeared in chat
5. GitHub account access request card appeared — Selected GitHub account (already checked) and clicked "Allow Access (1)" to grant permission
6. Waited for response — Agent processed for 2m 35s, requesting multiple GitHub API permissions during execution

7. Agent returned response but without GitHub username — Response indicated 401 authentication error. Agent stated: "The proxy is returning 401 — the GitHub OAuth token isn't being injected properly when reaching the GitHub API." Agent offered workaround options instead of returning the requested GitHub username. Task requirement not met: response should have included GitHub username but instead showed authentication failure.
bugs: GitHub OAuth authentication failed: Agent received "401 Bad credentials" error when attempting to call GitHub API endpoint (api.github.com/user) through the platform proxy. The injected OAuth token was not valid or not being injected properly into the GitHub REST API requests, preventing the agent from successfully retrieving the authenticated user's GitHub username.
